root_text.an6.19:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.an6.19:1.6: “maraṇassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā.
root_text.an6.19:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.19:2.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:2.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ rattindivaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:2.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:3.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:3.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ divasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:3.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:4.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:4.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:4.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:5.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:5.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ cattāro pañca ālope saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:5.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:6.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:6.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekaṁ ālopaṁ saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:6.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:7.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, bhāvesi maraṇassatin”ti?
root_text.an6.19:7.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ assasitvā vā passasāmi passasitvā vā assasāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:7.6: Evaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhāvemi maraṇassatin”ti.
root_text.an6.19:8.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā te bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.an6.19:8.2: “yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:8.3: ‘aho vatāhaṁ rattindivaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:9.1: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:9.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ divasaṁ jīveyyaṁ, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:10.1: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:10.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekapiṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:11.1: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:11.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ cattāro pañca ālope saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:12.1: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, bhikkhū pamattā viharanti dandhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāventi āsavānaṁ khayāya.
root_text.an6.19:13.1: Yo ca khvāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:13.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ ekaṁ ālopaṁ saṅkhāditvā ajjhoharāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:14.1: Yo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāveti:
root_text.an6.19:14.2: ‘aho vatāhaṁ tadantaraṁ jīveyyaṁ yadantaraṁ assasitvā vā passasāmi passasitvā vā assasāmi, bhagavato sāsanaṁ manasi kareyyaṁ, bahu vata me kataṁ assā’ti.
root_text.an6.19:15.1: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, bhikkhū appamattā viharanti tikkhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāventi āsavānaṁ khayāya.
root_text.an6.19:16.2: ‘appamattā viharissāma, tikkhaṁ maraṇassatiṁ bhāvessāma āsavānaṁ khayāyā’ti.

translation_text.an6.19:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.an6.19:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an6.19:1.6: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of death is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial. It has freedom from death as its objective and culmination.
translation_text.an6.19:1.7: But do you develop mindfulness of death?”
translation_text.an6.19:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:2.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:2.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live for another day and night, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:2.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:3.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:3.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:3.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:3.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live for another day, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:3.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:4.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:4.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:4.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:4.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to eat a meal of almsfood, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:4.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:5.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:5.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:5.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:5.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to chew and swallow four or five mouthfuls, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:5.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:6.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:6.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:6.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:6.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to chew and swallow a single mouthful, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:6.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:7.1: Another mendicant said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.19:7.2: “Sir, I too develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:7.3: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”
translation_text.an6.19:7.5: ‘Oh, if I’d only live as long as it takes to breathe out after breathing in, or to breathe in after breathing out, I’d focus on the Buddha’s instructions and I could really achieve a lot.’
translation_text.an6.19:7.6: That’s how I develop mindfulness of death.”
translation_text.an6.19:8.1: When this was said, the Buddha said to those mendicants:
translation_text.an6.19:8.2: “As to the mendicants who develop mindfulness of death by wishing
translation_text.an6.19:8.3: to live for a day and night …
translation_text.an6.19:9.2: or to live for a day …
translation_text.an6.19:10.2: or to live as long as it takes to eat a meal of almsfood …
translation_text.an6.19:11.2: or to live as long as it takes to chew and swallow four or five mouthfuls—
translation_text.an6.19:12.1: these are called mendicants who live negligently. They slackly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an6.19:13.1: But as to the mendicants who develop mindfulness of death by wishing
translation_text.an6.19:13.2: to live as long as it takes to chew and swallow a single mouthful …
translation_text.an6.19:14.2: or to live as long as it takes to breathe out after breathing in, or to breathe in after breathing out—
translation_text.an6.19:15.1: these are called mendicants who live diligently. They keenly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an6.19:16.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an6.19:16.2: ‘We will live diligently. We will keenly develop mindfulness of death for the ending of defilements.’
